title: Malapoenna tomentosa

This family is part of the larger clade of eurosids, specifically within the order Malpighiales. The Malpighiaceae family, in turn, falls under the broader category of flowering plants known as angiosperms. intro:
I couldn't find any information about "Malapoenna tomentosa", however, I found "Malpighia emarginata" which is also known as Malapoenna, or Malapai. Here is a paragraph about Malpighia emarginata:

Malpighia emarginata, also known as Malapoenna, is a plant species in the genus Malpighia, which is a type of shrub or small tree native to the tropical regions of the Americas. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it useful in treating various ailments such as fever, wounds, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Malpighia emarginata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and anthocyanins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Malpighia emarginata is commonly prepared as a decoction or tea, as well as being used in traditional remedies such as infusions and tinctures.
